data.title: Sanofi faces stock decline as CEO change announced, market cap of $113.6B, shares down 4.5%, near 52-week low.
data.description: Sanofi SA (NASDAQ: SNY) shares are experiencing a decline in the premarket session on Friday due to leadership changes amidst ongoing issues in its drug pipeline. The French drugmaker, with a market cap of approximately $113.6 billion, closed 4.5% lower on Thursday, moving closer to the lower end of its 52-week range of $44.62-$60.12. The departure of CEO Paul Hudson was announced by the board, with Belén Garijo set to succeed him. Hudson's last day as CEO is scheduled for February 17, and Garijo will assume the role after the Annual General Meeting on April 29. Sanofi aims to strengthen its strategy and innovation capacity, particularly in Research & Development. In the fourth quarter of 2025, Sanofi saw a significant 32.2% increase in sales of its leading drug, Dupixent, amounting to 4.2 billion euros.

data.title: Citius Oncology (NASDAQ: CTOR) Q1 earnings missed by -50%, EPS of $-0.06 vs. $-0.04 estimate, revenue increased by $3.94M.
data.description: Citius Oncology (NASDAQ: CTOR) released its Q1 earnings report on February 13, 2026, at 08:00 AM. The company fell short of estimated earnings by -50.0%, with an EPS of $-0.06 compared to the expected $-0.04. Revenue increased by $3.94 million from the prior year's same quarter. For more details on the earnings history and the complete story, refer to the article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: ReGrow Israel partners with Oracle to analyze data for agriculture recovery; enhances farming resilience.
data.description: In Austin, Texas on February 13, 2026, ReGrow Israel announced their decision to utilize Oracle Agriculture Data Intelligence in a bid to enhance agricultural practices. This integration of technology aims to analyze extensive datasets for forecasting crop yield and identifying potential risk factors. The strategic partnership intends to revitalize and bolster farming initiatives, signaling a proactive approach towards sustainable agriculture.

data.title: Ecovyst to host Q4 2025 earnings call on Feb 26, 2026 at 11:00 a.m. ET. NYSE: ECVT. Virg. sulfuric acid provider.
data.description: On February 13, 2026, Ecovyst Inc. (NYSE: ECVT) based in Wayne, Pennsylvania, a prominent supplier of virgin sulfuric acid, sulfuric acid regeneration, and ex-situ catalyst activation services, revealed plans to host a conference call and audio webcast on Thursday. The company specializes in these services and is well-known in the industry. This announcement indicates the importance of the upcoming event for investors, stakeholders, and anyone interested in the company's activities. The call will provide valuable insights into Ecovyst's operations, performance, and future outlook. It presents a significant opportunity for individuals to gain a comprehensive understanding of the company's strategies and potential growth.

data.title: The Marketing Alliance reported Q3 FY2026 financial results: $5,018,127 revenue, $299,637 operating income, $268,692 net income, repurchased 117,962 shares this quarter, and 295,959 shares post-quarter.
data.description: The Marketing Alliance, Inc. (TMA) reported its financial results for the fiscal 2026 third quarter, ended December 31, 2025. The company saw a revenue increase of over 10% to $5,018,127, compared to $4,550,421 in the previous year. Operating income from continuing operations improved significantly to $299,637 from $(124,345) in the prior year quarter. Net income for the quarter was $268,692, or $0.04 per share, compared to a loss of $(164,867) or $(0.02) per share in the prior year quarter. The company also repurchased 117,962 shares during the quarter and an additional 295,959 shares post-quarter end as of January 31, 2026. TMA's balance sheet as of December 31, 2025, showed cash and cash equivalents of $1.4 million, working capital of $5.5 million, and shareholders' equity of $5.8 million. The company has been focusing on investments in the insurance business, which contributed to the positive results, while the construction business faced challenges due to delays at a large project affecting revenues. Management expressed satisfaction with the quarter's performance and highlighted ongoing efforts to enhance operations and communication with agencies.

data.title: Columbia Banking System declares $0.37 dividend per common share, payable on March 16, 2026.
data.description: In Tacoma, Washington on February 13, 2026, the parent company of Columbia Bank, Columbia Banking System, Inc. (Nasdaq: COLB), disclosed that its Board of Directors has sanctioned a quarterly cash dividend of $0.37 per common share. This dividend is set to be distributed on March 16, 2026 to shareholders.

data.title: Nephros expands into Puerto Rico to provide water filtration solutions in medical, commercial sectors.
data.description: Nephros has recently entered the Puerto Rican market to provide unique water filtration solutions within the medical and commercial industries. This expansion allows Nephros to offer their innovative products to a new customer base, enhancing their reach and impact. The company's specialized filtration systems are designed to provide high-quality water for various uses, catering specifically to the needs of medical facilities and commercial establishments. By expanding into Puerto Rico, Nephros aims to address the growing demand for reliable water filtration solutions in the region. This move signifies a significant step for Nephros as they continue to grow their presence globally and increase access to their cutting-edge technology.

data.title: Astrotech's Q2 FY2026 financial results show $1,832k in research & development expenses, with TRACER 1000 deployed in 35 locations in 16 countries. CEO expects strong growth and global scale. Subsidiaries include 1st Detect, AgLAB, Pro-Control, and BreathTech among others.
data.description: Astrotech Corporation reported its financial results for the second quarter of fiscal year 2026, with a decline in research and development expenses to $1,832 thousand, a 25% decrease from the same period in fiscal year 2025. The company has deployed the TRACER 1000 trace detection system in around 35 locations across 16 countries. Astrotech's Chairman and CEO, Thomas B. Pickens, III, stated that the company is experiencing positive momentum in its sales pipeline globally. The company has strengthened its team with the appointment of Scott Bartley as Interim CFO and David Spada as Director of Global Sales. Astrotech Corporation is headquartered in Austin, Texas, operating through subsidiaries like 1st Detect, AgLAB, Pro-Control, and BreathTech, serving specialized markets. The company's financial highlights included revenue of $148 thousand in the second quarter of fiscal year 2026, with operating expenses and gross profit detailed in the financial tables provided in the full article. The company acknowledges the risks and uncertainties in the market and the evolving regulatory environment and emphasizes the importance of evaluating forward-looking statements in light of these factors. Further information and financial tables are available on Benzinga.com. Investors and media contacts are provided for further inquiries.

data.title: Citius Oncology (CTOR) Q1 earnings announced: EPS -$0.06 missed by -50.0%. Revenue up $3.94 million YoY. Details on Benzinga.com.
data.description: On Friday, February 13, 2026, Citius Oncology (NASDAQ: CTOR) released its Q1 earnings report at 04:00 PM. The company fell short of estimated earnings by -50.0%, revealing an EPS of $-0.06 compared to the projected $-0.04. Revenue saw a significant increase, rising by $3.94 million from the previous year. For more details on Citius Oncology's earnings history, visit the full article on Benzinga.com.
